children.name: Sanderia
children.description: Sanderia — a genus of pelagiid jellyfish in the family Pelagiidae.

children.name: Sanderia malayensis
children.description: Sanderia malayensis is a species of jellyfish in the family Pelagiidae, native to the tropical Indo-Pacific.

children.name: Sanderia pampinosus
children.description: Sanderia pampinosus is a disc jellyfish in the family Pelagiidae from seas off northern Australia.

children.name: Chrysaora achlyos
children.description: Chrysaora achlyos, the black sea nettle, sometimes informally known as the black jellyfish, is a species of jellyfish that can be found in the waters of the Pacific Ocean off North America.

children.name: Chrysaora chinensis
children.description: Chrysaora chinensis, the Indonesian sea nettle or Malaysian Sea Nettle, is a species of jellyfish in the family Pelagiidae.

children.name: Chrysaora colorata
children.description: Chrysaora colorata (Russell), commonly known as the purple-striped jelly or purple-striped sea nettle, is a species of jellyfish that exists primarily off the coast of California from Bodega Bay to San Diego.

children.name: Chrysaora melanaster
children.description: Chrysaora melanaster, commonly known as the northern sea nettle or brown jellyfish, is a species of jellyfish native to the northern Pacific Ocean and adjacent parts of the Arctic Ocean.

children.name: Chrysaora pacifica
children.description: Chrysaora pacifica, commonly named the Japanese sea nettle, is a jellyfish in the family Pelagiidae.

children.description: Chrysaora pentastoma or Australian Sea Nettle is a species of jellyfish from the family Pelagiidae.

children.name: Chrysaora plocamia

children.name: Chrysaora quinquecirrha
children.description: thumb|Atlantic sea nettle (Chrysaora quinquecirrha) swimming at the Monterey Bay Aquarium The Atlantic sea nettle (Chrysaora quinquecirrha), also called the East Coast sea nettle in the United States, is a species of jellyfish that inhabits the Atlantic coast of the United States.
